curation.synopsis: Seven Warhammer Horror stories collected as The Harrowed Paths, ranging from a colonel's haunted monograph and a planet that registers only as a ghost on the auspex to a sketch-artist drawn into the work of a serial killer and an old woman whose last candle gutters under the weight of a necroquake. Voices in mosaic tile, a cutter who works in bone, and a quiet drowning under dark water round out the collection. The book travels between 40K hive cities and the Mortal Realms without ever quite settling, and the throughline is less a setting than a tone — the patient, attentive horror of being noticed by something you should have left undisturbed.

flags.reason: The Harrowed Paths is a seven-story Warhammer Horror anthology (McNeill, Gray, Strachan, Sheil, Kyme, Hinks, Ozga), not a single-author novel; Graham McNeill is the contributing author of one story
Anthology spans 40K hive cities and AoS Mortal Realms; per-story aggregation not reliably surfaced in this pass
